channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Council grass cutting mess left behind today, 31st March
item.description:
Today the grass area has been cut at the far end of Evergreen Close. Disgraceful mess of grass cuttings and shredded litter strewn all over the footpaths between the grass areas. Looks unsightly!!!! Please can the job be finished properly and the mess be cleared up left on the footpaths of Evergreen Close. Thankyou...
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Evergreen Close, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Growing Pothole in middle of junction, 31st March
item.description:
A few week old, growing every week.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Townsend Avenue, Southgate
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Wide, Growing Pothole, 31st March
item.description:
This pothole has been here for a few weeks now and is growing and becoming dangerous.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Forestdale, Southgate
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish bags strewn over street, 31st March
item.description:
See above

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): King George Street, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Walkway 2-18 Penton place SE17 3JT, 31st March
item.description:
I would like to report repeated dog fouling outside my property. The owner consistently fails to clean up after their dog despite previous reports. This issue is ongoing and causing hygiene concerns. Please take appropriate action.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 4, Penton Place, Elephant and Castle, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Persistent dog waste dropped, 31st March
item.description:
This has been going on for a month or so whereby a dog walker is dropping their dogs meds at the back of this street container. They mutually I was clearing it as it’s against my fence but it’s becoming more of a nuisance and the bags are building up.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Westwood Lane, Blackfen, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Various household rubbish dumped on pavement, 31st March
item.description:
Rubbish on pavement

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 90, De Laune Street, Kennington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: This tree needs significant pruning, 31st March
item.description:
This tree has grown far too big and tall and whenever it is pruned the contractors only take a few of the bottom branches off - they hardly cut it at all. Over the 30 years that I have lived at the property which it is in front of, it has grown 10-fold and has never been kept under control. The roots are spreading far too wide and they have damaged the pavement causing issues for elderly people walking along. I have also had a building survey carried out at my property and have been informed that this tree's roots are causing cracks in my house. This urgently needs attention before significant damage is done to my property, for which I will be suing the Council if preventative action is not taken. This tree should be pruned on a regular basis and when the contractors are employed to do it, you need to ensure that they actually carry out the job properly as they have not done so in the past.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 63, Dulverton Road, New Eltham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 31st March
item.description:
The dumped rubbish has been left since Sunday 29th March on a public pavement outside 138 Ravensbury Road

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 120, Ravensbury Road, St Mary Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter still remains in situ at location, 31st March
item.description:
Thank you for removing the glass and some litter from this area which was previously reported via Fix my Street on 26.03.2026. However the litter previously reported (please see photos) still remains at the side and the rear of the large commercial bin and between the bin and BT box at the side of the Candy Box in Maple Crescent Please can you ensure that the cleaning operatives tasked with removing this litter do an effective job. Thank you

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Maple Crescent, Blackfen, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: F, 31st March
item.description:
Graffiti on wall of Cherry Park Gardens SE164TT

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 170, Bermondsey Wall East, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipping and rubbish next to traid clothing collection bank, brooksby walk, 31st March
item.description:
Flytipping and rubbish next to the TRAID clothing collection bank situated on Brooksby Walk (near Homerton Grove Adventure Playground)
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Elderfield Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A raised paving slab outside the entrance to 160 Victoria Street, 31st March
item.description:
A raised paving slab outside the entrance to 160 Victoria Street causing a trip hazard.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 150, Victoria Street, Victoria, City of Westminster
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Pavement Defect (uneven surface / cracked paving slab)

item.title: Extensive deep potholes on Lower Road, 31st March
item.description:
Large, deep potholes along Lower Road immediately after the junction with Cope Street. The defects are spread across multiple areas, including the middle of the carriageway. Drivers are forced to take evasive actions, creating a significant risk of collisions. Multiple reports have been submitted over the last two months, but no action has been taken. This is a critical road with heavy daily traffic, and urgent inspection and repair are required to prevent accidents
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 138, Lower Road, Rotherhithe, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dropping poisonous berries, 31st March
item.description:
My 2 year old picked up some berries from the tree and ended up in hospital

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Woodbine Grove, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dangerously damaged speed bumps on John Ruskin Street, 31st March
item.description:
Multiple damaged speed bumps on John Ruskin Street near John Ruskin Primary School. Vehicles unexpectedly drop when crossing, even at low speeds. One speed bump is immediately before a sunken manhole cover, increasing risk of vehicle damage, loss of control, and collisions. This is a school zone with heavy traffic, putting drivers, cyclists, and students at risk. Urgent inspection and repair of all 3–4 damaged speed bumps is required to prevent accidents and congestion.”
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): John Ruskin Street, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Glass window panes opposite 93 elderfield rd, e5, 31st March
item.description:
Flytipping of glass window panes opposite 93 Elderfield Rd, E5 (near Rushmore Primary School). This needs URGENT attention
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Elderfield Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Traffic chaos, 31st March
item.description:
You can't really have a four wide temporary light on commercial way which are currently stuck on red. This is caused unnecessary traffic. Potential accidents have nearly taken place This morning due to everyone trying to get through the temporary lights, which are currently having stuck on red for a few hours. There are currently no workmen working on this road where the temporary lights are currently up. This needs to be dealt with quickly. This part of the road has already been dug up recently. And why are there roadworks with temporary lights? An absolutely no workmen, doing any work. If they were there on site, the temperate lights we'll probably be fixed.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Southampton Way, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: More fly tipping here and litter spilling into street., 31st March
item.description:
The street is perpetually filthy. The continual flytipping doesn’t help but the street is never swept and full of general litter. It looks awful.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Westcombe Hill, Blackheath
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Illegal encampment, 31st March
item.description:
Furniture, fire, tent material. Looks mostly abandoned but quite bad. Requires significant work to remove all items. Might have people back during dry weather. Risk of fire and who knows what else.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Admaston Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet
